（内部に刳抜式[[家形石棺]]） |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]前半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 国の[[史跡]]「愛宕山古墳」
（「総社古墳群」に包含） |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |アイコン = 方墳 |ラベル = 総社愛宕山
古墳 |ラベル位置 = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250| height =300 | caption = {{center|総社古墳群分布図}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1= {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1=遠見山 | label-pos1=top | label-color1=black | mark1=前方後円墳-南西.png | mark-size1=30 | mark-coord2= {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2=王山 | label-pos2=left | label-color2=black | mark2=前方後円墳-北.png | mark-size2=30 | mark-coord3= {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3=二子山 | label-pos3=top | label-color3=black | mark3=前方後円墳-西北西.png | mark-size3=30 | mark-coord4= {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4=愛宕山 | label-pos4=bottom | label-color4=black | mark4=方墳.png | mark-size4=30 | mark-coord5= {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5=宝塔山 | label-pos5=left | label-color5=black | mark5=方墳.png | mark-size5=30 | mark-coord6= {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6=蛇穴山 | label-pos6=bottom | label-color6=black | mark6=方墳.png | mark-size6=30 }} '''総社愛宕山古墳'''（そうじゃあたごやまこふん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]]総社町総社にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[方墳]]。総社古墳群を構成する古墳の1つ。国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「愛宕山古墳」、史跡「総社古墳群」のうち）[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/93955201.html 文化審議会の答申（史跡名勝天然記念物の指定等）について]（文化庁報道発表 、2023年10月20日）。令和6年2月21日文部科学省告示第14号。。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+総社古墳群7基 !支群 !古墳名 !形状 !規模 !埋葬施設 !築造時期 !史跡指定 |- |北||[[遠見山古墳]]||[[前方後円墳]]||墳丘長88m||[[竪穴式石室]]?||5c後半||市史跡 |- |rowspan=2|南||[[王山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長76m||[[横穴式石室]]||6c初頭||国史跡 |- |王河原山古墳?||前方後円墳|| || || ||（消滅） |- |rowspan=4|北||[[総社二子山古墳|二子山古墳]]||前方後円墳||墳丘長90m||横穴式石室2基||6c後半||国史跡 |- |'''愛宕山古墳'''||[[方墳]]||一辺56m||横穴式石室||7c前半||国史跡 |- |[[宝塔山古墳]]||方墳||一辺66m||複室横穴式石室||7c中葉||国史跡 |- |[[蛇穴山古墳]]||方墳||一辺44m||横穴式石室||7c後半||国史跡 |}
群馬県中部、[[榛名山]]東南麓・[[利根川]]西岸の前橋台地上に築造された大型方墳である。[[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）に測量調査が、[[1995年]]（[[平成]]7年）以降に発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は方形で（かつては円形と推定）、一辺56メートル・高さ8.5メートルを測る{{Sfn|東国の雄 総社古墳群（パンフレット）|2016}}。墳丘は3段築成以上（かつては2段築成と推定）{{Sfn|令和3年2月 愛宕山古墳現地説明会資料|2021}}。墳丘外表には[[葺石]]が認められるが、[[埴輪]]は認められていない{{Sfn|東国の雄 総社古墳群（パンフレット）|2016}}。また墳丘周囲には周濠が巡らされる{{Sfn|令和3年2月 愛宕山古墳現地説明会資料|2021}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。石室内には[[凝灰岩]]製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据えており、群馬県内では家形石棺は愛宕山古墳・[[宝塔山古墳]]のほか[[今泉口八幡山古墳]]（[[太田市]]）の古墳3基が知られるのみであるとして注目される。石室内は未調査のため副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]前半頃と推定される{{Sfn|東国の雄 総社古墳群（パンフレット）|2016}}。総社古墳群では[[総社二子山古墳|二子山古墳]]に後続し、宝塔山古墳に先行する時期に位置づけられる。総社古墳群のうち愛宕山・宝塔山・蛇穴山の大型方墳3基は、前方後円墳終焉後の上毛野地方において他古墳とは一線を画する規模・内容になる。総社地域が古代上毛野地方（[[上野国]]）の政治的・文化的中心となり、上野国府・[[上野国分寺跡|上野国分寺]]・[[上野国分尼寺跡|上野国分尼寺]]の成立を導く過程を考察するうえで重要視される古墳になる。 古墳域は[[2024年]]（[[令和]]6年）に国の[[史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「愛宕山古墳」、史跡「総社古墳群」のうち）。 Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funcho.jpg|墳頂
{{small|背景は前橋市立第六中学校。}} Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘北西隅 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1988年]]（[[昭和]]63年）、測量調査（右島和夫ら、1988年に報告）{{Sfn|総社愛宕山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * [[1995年]]度（[[平成]]7年度）、市立中学校移転新築事業に伴う愛宕山遺跡の調査。愛宕山古墳の周濠を確認、方墳と確定（前橋市埋蔵文化財発掘調査団、[[1996年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|総社愛宕山遺跡|1996}}。 * [[2020年]]度（[[令和]]2年度）、範囲確認調査（前橋市教育委員会、[[2023年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|令和3年2月 愛宕山古墳現地説明会資料|2021}}。 * [[2024年]]（令和6年）2月21日、国の[[史跡]]に指定（指定名称は「愛宕山古墳」、史跡「総社古墳群」のうち）。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-C.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石棺立断面図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|総社愛宕山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 * 石室全長：9.3メートル * 玄室：長さ約7メートル、幅3メートル（奥壁）、高さ2.8メートル 石室の石材には自然石の巨石が使用され、部分的に加工が認められる{{Sfn|東国の雄 総社古墳群（パンフレット）|2016}}。玄室の奥壁は墳丘の中心にあたる{{Sfn|令和3年2月 愛宕山古墳現地説明会資料|2021}}。羨道の大部分は土砂で埋没する。 玄室内の奥部には、凝灰岩製刳抜式[[家形石棺]]を据える。石棺主軸は石室主軸と直交する。大型の石棺であり、棺身外法は長辺2.22メートル・短辺1.18メートル・高さ1.02メートル、棺蓋外法は長辺2.26メートル・短辺1.23メートル・高さ0.50メートルを測る{{Sfn|総社愛宕山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。棺蓋の長辺には縄掛突起2対を造り出す{{Sfn|総社愛宕山古墳（続古墳）|2002}}。 群馬県内では家形石棺を使用しているのは、総社古墳群の愛宕山古墳・宝塔山古墳、[[今泉口八幡山古墳]]（[[太田市]]）の古墳3基のみになる[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html 総社・清里の文化財を訪ねて > 愛宕山古墳]（前橋市ホームページ）。。 Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|玄室の家形石棺 Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|家形石棺内部 Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|開口部 Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-B.jpg|石棺俯瞰図 Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|石棺展開図 == 文化財 == === 国の史跡 === * 愛宕山古墳（史跡「総社古墳群」のうち） - 2024年（令和6年）2月21日指定。 == 関連施設 == * 前橋市総社歴史資料館（前橋市総社町総社） == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（総社地区史跡愛存会、1998年設置） * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf 「東国の雄 総社古墳群」（パンフレット）]（前橋市教育委員会事務局文化財保護課、2016年）|ref={{Harvid|東国の雄 総社古墳群（パンフレット）|2016}}}}。 - {{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Soja Atagoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side

(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early [[7th century]] | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early [[7th century]], located on the southeastern foothills of Mount [[Haruna]] and west of the [[Tone River]]. Survey and excavation of the site began in [[1988]] and continued from [[1995]] onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no [[haniwa]] were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-style stone chambers|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, [[Hotoyama Kofun]], and three mounds in [[Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. {{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Soja Atagoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early [[7th century]] | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early [[7th century]], located on the southeastern foothills of Mount [[Haruna]] and west of the [[Tone River]]. Survey and excavation of the site began in [[1988]] and continued from [[1995]] onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no [[haniwa]] were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-style stone chambers|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, [[Hotoyama Kofun]], and three mounds in [[Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]]

At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]]

{{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Soja Atagoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early [[7th century]] | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early [[7th century]], located on the southeastern foothills of Mount [[Haruna]] and west of the [[Tone River]]. Survey and excavation of the site began in [[1988]] and continued from [[1995]] onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no [[haniwa]] were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-style stone chambers|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, [[Hotoyama Kofun]], and three mounds in [[Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]]

In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun of Japan | Name = Soja Atagoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early [[7th century]] | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early [[7th century]], located on the southeastern foothills of Mount [[Haruna]] and west of the [[Tone River]]. Survey and excavation of the site began in [[1988]] and continued from [[1995]] onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no [[haniwa]] were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, [[Hotoyama Kofun]], and three mounds in [[Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[house-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]]

At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Infobox Kofun" to "Infobox ancient site"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Soja Atagoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early [[7th century]] | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early [[7th century]], located on the southeastern foothills of Mount [[Haruna]] and west of the [[Tone River]]. Survey and excavation of the site began in [[1988]] and continued from [[1995]] onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no [[haniwa]] were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, [[Hotoyama Kofun]], and three mounds in [[Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]]

At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Soja Atagoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early [[7th century]] | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early [[7th century]], located on the southeastern foothills of Mount [[Haruna]] and west of the [[Tone River]]. Survey and excavation of the site began in [[1988]] and continued from [[1995]] onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no [[haniwa]] were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, [[Hotoyama Kofun]], and three mounds in [[Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]]

The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:群馬県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]]

Kofun | LabelPosition = top }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, [[Maebashi]], [[Gunma Prefecture]], Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early [[7th century]], located on the southeastern foothills of Mount [[Haruna]] and west of the [[Tone River]]. Survey and excavation of the site began in [[1988]] and continued from [[1995]] onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no [[haniwa]] were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, [[Hotoyama Kofun]], and three mounds in [[Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no [[haniwa]] were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, [[Hotoyama Kofun]], and three mounds in [[Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting [[:en:Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|Kōzuke Kokubun-ji]] == Excavation History == * [[1988]]: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * [[1995]]: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * [[2024]]: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): A11roximately 7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:OSM on jawiki]] [[Category:Kofun in Gunma Prefecture]] [[Category:Historic Sites in Gunma]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Archaeological sites in Maebashi]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] }} [[sn:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]] [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}}
