Kofun | LabelPosition = top | coordinates = {{Coord|36.410194|N|139.036111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, located on the southeastern foothills of Mount {{ill|Haruna|en|Haruna|WD=Q1587857|lt=Haruna}} and west of the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}}. Survey and excavation of the site began in {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988}} and continued from {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}} onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|en|Hōtōzan Kofun|WD=Q17217330|lt=Hōtōzan Kofun}}, and three mounds in [[:en:Ota City|Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[:en:Kozuke Province|Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting {{ill|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|en|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|WD=Q17128356|lt=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988}}: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * {{ill|2024|en|2024|WD=Q49619|lt=2024}}: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): Approximately7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ota City|Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17222958}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|en|Hōtōzan Kofun|WD=Q17217330|lt=Hōtōzan Kofun}}, and three mounds in [[:en:Ota City|Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[:en:Kozuke Province|Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting {{ill|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|en|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|WD=Q17128356|lt=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988}}: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * {{ill|2024|en|2024|WD=Q49619|lt=2024}}: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): Approximately7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ota City|Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17222958}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Soja Atagoyama Kofun]][[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]][[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Soja Atagoyama Kofun]][[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]][[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Soja Atagoyama Kofun]][[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]][[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Soja Atagoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}} | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top | coordinates = {{Coord|36.410194|N|139.036111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century}}, located on the southeastern foothills of Mount {{ill|Haruna|en|Haruna|WD=Q1587857|lt=Haruna}} and west of the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River}}. Survey and excavation of the site began in {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988}} and continued from {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}} onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}} were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|en|Hōtōzan Kofun|WD=Q17217330|lt=Hōtōzan Kofun}}, and three mounds in [[:en:Ota City|Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[:en:Kozuke Province|Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting {{ill|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|en|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|WD=Q17128356|lt=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988}}: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995}}: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * {{ill|2024|en|2024|WD=Q49619|lt=2024}}: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): Approximately7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ota City|Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17222958}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top | coordinates = {{Coord|36.410194|N|139.036111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, located on the southeastern foothills of Mount {{ill|Haruna|en|Haruna|WD=Q1587857|lt=Haruna|1=Haruna}} and west of the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}}. Survey and excavation of the site began in {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} and continued from {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|en|Hōtōzan Kofun|WD=Q17217330|lt=Hōtōzan Kofun|1=Hōtōzan Kofun}}, and three mounds in [[:en:Ota City|Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[:en:Kozuke Province|Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting {{ill|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|en|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|WD=Q17128356|lt=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji|1=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}}: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * {{ill|2024|en|2024|WD=Q49619|lt=2024|1=2024}}: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): Approximately7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ota City|Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17222958}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Survey and excavation of the site began in {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} and continued from {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|en|Hōtōzan Kofun|WD=Q17217330|lt=Hōtōzan Kofun|1=Hōtōzan Kofun}}, and three mounds in [[:en:Ota City|Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[:en:Kozuke Province|Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting {{ill|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|en|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|WD=Q17128356|lt=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji|1=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}}: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * {{ill|2024|en|2024|WD=Q49619|lt=2024|1=2024}}: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): Approximately7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imaizumiguchi Hachimanyama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ota City|Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17222958}} [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top | coordinates = {{Coord|36.410194|N|139.036111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, located on the southeastern foothills of Mount {{ill|Haruna|en|Haruna|WD=Q1587857|lt=Haruna|1=Haruna}} and west of the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}}. Survey and excavation of the site began in {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} and continued from {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|en|Hōtōzan Kofun|WD=Q17217330|lt=Hōtōzan Kofun|1=Hōtōzan Kofun}}, and three mounds in [[:en:Ota City|Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[:en:Kozuke Province|Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting {{ill|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|en|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|WD=Q17128356|lt=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji|1=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}}: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * {{ill|2024|en|2024|WD=Q49619|lt=2024|1=2024}}: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): Approximately7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Ima Izumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Izumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ota City|Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17222958}} [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Ima Izumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Izumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ota City|Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17222958}} [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Soja Atagoyama Kofun]][[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]][[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Soja Atagoyama Kofun]][[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]][[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] [[en:Sōja Atagoyama Kofun]][[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site of Japan | Name = Soja Atagoyama Kofun | image = [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Mound (stone chamber opening in the center back) | AlternateName = Soja Town No. 10 Kofun | part_of = Soja Kofun Cluster | Location = Soja Town, Soja, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan | LatitudeDegrees = 36 | LatitudeMinutes = 24 | LatitudeSeconds = 36.40 | LongitudeDegrees = 139 | LongitudeMinutes = 2 | LongitudeSeconds = 10.32 | ISO = JP-10 | Shape = [[Square Kofun]] | Dimensions = 56m per side
8.5m high | burial = Double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]]
(containing a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]]) | Artifacts = | AccompanyingBurials = | ConstructionPeriod = Early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}} | BurialPerson = | Mausoleum = | CulturalProperties = National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] "Atagoyama Kofun"
(part of "Soja Kofun Cluster") | NotableFeatures = | Map = Japan Gunma | Icon = Square Kofun | Label = Soja Atagoyama
Kofun | LabelPosition = top | coordinates = {{Coord|36.410194|N|139.036111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} {{OSM Location map | coord = {{Coord|36|24|12|N|139|2|36|E}} | zoom = 14 | float = right | width = 250 | height = 300 | caption = {{center|Distribution map of the Soja Kofun Cluster}} | scalemark = 60 | mark-coord1 = {{Coord|36|24|34.73|N|139|2|37.45|E}} | label1 = Tomiyama | label-pos1 = top | label-color1 = black | mark1 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size1 = 30 | mark-coord2 = {{Coord|36|23|41.80|N|139|3|10.75|E}} | label2 = Oyamayama | label-pos2 = left | label-color2 = black | mark2 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size2 = 30 | mark-coord3 = {{Coord|36|24|41.40|N|139|2|5.05|E}} | label3 = Futagoyama | label-pos3 = top | label-color3 = black | mark3 = Keyhole Kofun.png | mark-size3 = 30 | mark-coord4 = {{Coord|36|24|36.40|N|139|2|10.32|E}} | label4 = Atagoyama | label-pos4 = bottom | label-color4 = black | mark4 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size4 = 30 | mark-coord5 = {{Coord|36|24|26.35|N|139|2|18.80|E}} | label5 = Hotoyama | label-pos5 = left | label-color5 = black | mark5 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size5 = 30 | mark-coord6 = {{Coord|36|24|24.55|N|139|2|23.45|E}} | label6 = Janazuyama | label-pos6 = bottom | label-color6 = black | mark6 = Square Kofun.png | mark-size6 = 30 }} The '''Soja Atagoyama Kofun''' (総社愛宕山古墳) is an ancient [[kofun]] Kofun located in Soja Town, {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi|1=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture|1=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan. It is a square-shaped mound and part of the Soja Kofun Cluster. It has been designated a National [[:en:Historic Site|Historic Site]] as part of the "Soja Kofun Cluster."[https://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodoha11yo/93955201.html Agency for Cultural Affairs Announcement] (October 20, 2023).Ministry of Education, Culture, Sports, Science and Technology Notification No. 14, February 21, 2024. == Overview == The kofun is a large square-shaped Kofun constructed in the early {{ill|7th century|en|7th century|WD=Q8089|lt=7th century|1=7th century}}, located on the southeastern foothills of Mount {{ill|Haruna|en|Haruna|WD=Q1587857|lt=Haruna|1=Haruna}} and west of the {{ill|Tone River|en|Tone River|WD=Q177452|lt=Tone River|1=Tone River}}. Survey and excavation of the site began in {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}} and continued from {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}} onwards. The mound measures 56 meters per side and 8.5 meters high. The structure has three or more terraced steps and is surrounded by a moat. The outer surface of the mound is covered in [[fukiishi]], though no {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} were discovered.[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/material/files/group/94/soujakohun.pdf "Soja Kofun Cluster" Pamphlet], Maebashi City Board of Education, 2016. The burial chamber is a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|Yokoana-style Stone Chamber]] with its entrance facing south. Inside, a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff rock is placed. The use of this type of sarcophagus is rare in Gunma Prefecture and is only found in the Atagoyama Kofun, {{ill|Hōtōzan Kofun|en|Hōtōzan Kofun|WD=Q17217330|lt=Hōtōzan Kofun|1=Hōtōzan Kofun}}, and three mounds in [[:en:Ota City|Ota City]]. == Historical Significance == The construction of large square Kofuns in the late [[Kofun period]] reflects a shift in burial customs, representing the political and cultural centralization in the [[:en:Kozuke Province|Kozuke Province]]. The Soja area became an important regional center, ultimately hosting {{ill|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|en|Kōzuke_Kokubun-ji|WD=Q17128356|lt=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji|1=Kōzuke Kokubun-ji}} == Excavation History == * {{ill|1988|en|1988|WD=Q2426|lt=1988|1=1988}}: Survey conducted by Kazuo Ushijima.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15449 "Soja Atagoyama Excavation Report"], Maebashi City Archaeological Team, 1996. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|WD=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}: Excavation confirming the moat and square shape of the mound.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/106161 Maebashi Archaeological Survey Report, 1996] * {{ill|2024|en|2024|WD=Q49619|lt=2024|1=2024}}: Designated as a National Historic Site. == Burial Facilities == [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead View of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, 3D-N-2.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Stone Chamber}}]] [[File:Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded Diagram of the Sarcophagus}}]] The burial facility consists of a double-sleeve [[Corridor-Style Stone Chamber]] with an opening facing south. The dimensions of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Total length: 9.3 meters * Burial chamber (gen-shitsu): Approximately7 meters long, 3 meters wide (rear wall), and 2.8 meters high The chamber is constructed using natural megalithic stones, with some partial modifications{{Sfn|The Hero of the Eastern Region: Soja Kofun Cluster (Pamphlet)|2016}}. The rear wall of the chamber aligns with the center of the Kofun{{Sfn|Atagoyama Kofun Fieldwork Materials, February Reiwa 3|2021}}. Most of the passageway (sendo) is buried under sediment. At the back of the burial chamber lies a carved [[House-shaped Sarcophagus|House-shaped sarcophagus]] made from tuff. The sarcophagus axis is perpendicular to the main axis of the chamber. It is a large sarcophagus with the following dimensions{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}: * Sarcophagus body (external dimensions): 2.22 meters long, 1.18 meters wide, 1.02 meters high * Sarcophagus lid (external dimensions): 2.26 meters long, 1.23 meters wide, 0.50 meters high Two pairs of rope-loop projections are carved into the long sides of the sarcophagus lid{{Sfn|Soja Atagoyama Kofun (Su11lementary Kofun)|2002}}. In Gunma Prefecture, the use of house-shaped sarcophagi is rare and limited to the Atagoyama Kofun, Hotoyama Kofun, and three mounds in {{ill|Ima Izumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|ja|今泉口八幡山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ima Izumiguchi Hachiman-yama Kofun|qq=}} ([[:en:Ota City|Ota City]])[https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Exploring the Cultural Properties of Soja and Kiyosato: Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website.. Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the rear wall) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-1.jpg|House-shaped sarcophagus in the burial chamber Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sekkan-2.jpg|Interior of the house-shaped sarcophagus Soja Atagoyama Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (view towards the entrance) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (view towards the burial chamber) Soja Atagoyama Kofun, entrance-2.jpg|Entrance to the chamber == Cultural Properties == === National Historic Site === * Atagoyama Kofun (part of the Soja Kofun Cluster) - Designated February 21, 2024. == Related Facilities == * Maebashi Soja Historical Museum (Soja Town, Maebashi City) == References == {{References}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/1/5116.html "Atagoyama Kofun"] - Maebashi City Official Website. ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|総社愛宕山古墳|version=103773454|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:総社愛宕山古墳]] {{Commons category|Soja Atagoyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17222958}} [[Category:History of Maebashi, Gunma]] [[Category:Kofun of Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:National historic sites in Gunma prefecture]] [[Category:Square Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
